question: What is a database Job (DBMS_SCHEDULER)?
options: A background process of the instance
A task scheduled to run automatically at defined times or intervals inside the database

explanation.uz: Job — bazada avtomatik, belgilangan vaqt yoki intervalda ishlaydigan vazifa (masalan tunda statistika yig'ish, hisobot). Zamonaviy Oracle'da DBMS_SCHEDULER bilan boshqariladi.
explanation.en: A job is a task that runs automatically at defined times or intervals inside the database (e.g. gather stats nightly, run a report). In modern Oracle it is managed with DBMS_SCHEDULER.

question: A job is scheduled every 5 minutes, but one run takes 10 minutes. What happens?
options: A second copy starts at the 5-minute mark, so two run at once
By default the same job does NOT overlap itself — the next run is deferred until the current one finishes
The job raises an error and stops
The database restarts the job twice

explanation.uz: Scheduler bir xil jobning nusxalarini bir vaqtda ishlatmaydi. Joriy ishga tushish 10 daqiqa davom etsa, oradagi 5-daqiqalik nuqta o'tkazib yuboriladi — keyingi ishga tushish joriysi tugagach rejalashtiriladi (o'zaro ustma-ust bo'lmaydi).
explanation.en: The Scheduler does not run overlapping instances of the same job. If a run takes 10 minutes, the intervening 5-minute mark is skipped — the next run is scheduled after the current one finishes (no self-overlap).

question: Can DIFFERENT jobs run in parallel (at the same time)?
options: No — only one job can run in the whole database at a time
Yes — different jobs run concurrently, limited by JOB_QUEUE_PROCESSES and resource plans

explanation.uz: Ha — turli joblar bir vaqtda parallel ishlaydi. Nechta parallel ishlashi JOB_QUEUE_PROCESSES parametri va resurs rejalari bilan cheklanadi. (Faqat bir xil jobning o'zi o'ziga ustma-ust ishlamaydi.)
explanation.en: Yes — different jobs run concurrently. How many run in parallel is limited by JOB_QUEUE_PROCESSES and resource plans. (Only the same job doesn't overlap itself.)

question: Which parameter limits how many scheduler/job processes can run concurrently?

explanation.uz: JOB_QUEUE_PROCESSES bir vaqtda ishga tushishi mumkin bo'lgan job jarayonlari sonini cheklaydi. 0 ga qo'yilsa, joblar umuman ishlamaydi.
explanation.en: JOB_QUEUE_PROCESSES caps how many job processes can run at once. If set to 0, jobs don't run at all.

question: How do you make sure a second instance of the SAME work does not start while one is still running?
options: Rely on the Scheduler (a repeating job never overlaps itself), or guard the logic with an application lock (DBMS_LOCK)

explanation.uz: Bir xil takrorlanuvchi job o'zi bilan ustma-ust ishlamaydi — bu tabiiy himoya. Agar mantiq turli joblardan yoki qo'lda ham chaqirilishi mumkin bo'lsa, DBMS_LOCK bilan qo'shimcha 'lock' qo'yib, ikkinchi nusxa boshlanmasligini kafolatlaysiz.
explanation.en: A repeating job never overlaps itself — that's built-in protection. If the same logic can also be triggered from other jobs or manually, add an application lock (DBMS_LOCK) so a second instance can't start.

question: What replaced the older DBMS_JOB package in modern Oracle?
options: DBMS_SCHEDULER — richer: calendar schedules, job chains, windows, resource plans, and logging

explanation.uz: DBMS_JOB o'rniga DBMS_SCHEDULER keldi — u kuchliroq: kalendar jadvallar, job zanjirlari (chains), oynalar (windows), resurs rejalari va batafsil log. Yangi tizimlarda DBMS_SCHEDULER tavsiya etiladi.
explanation.en: DBMS_SCHEDULER replaced DBMS_JOB — it is richer: calendar schedules, job chains, windows, resource plans, and detailed logging. New systems should use DBMS_SCHEDULER.
